channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two Teenage Girls Arrested Following Social Media Gathering in Clapham
item.description: On Tuesday evening, large groups of young people congregated on Clapham High Street, south London, following online posts encouraging a social media 'link-up'. The gathering caused significant disrupt...

item.title: Paul Makonda to attend 10th Kitambi Noma International Sports Bonanza in Arusha
item.description: For dis Easter Weekend, Arusha City go host the tenth edition of Kitambi Noma International sports bonanza, weh go run for three days. Di event don attract 20 teams from seven different regions of Tan...

item.title: Stars of 'Blood vs Duty' engage fans in interactive games on Kapamilya Chat
item.description: ABS-CBN's popular online show, Kapamilya Chat, recently hosted a special event featuring the cast of the hit series “Blood vs Duty.” Fans had the opportunity to participate in a day full of interactiv...

item.title: Escalating Tensions in the Gulf Threaten Global Oil Supply and Economic Stability
item.description: The article outlines an escalating crisis in global oil markets triggered by renewed US military action under Donald Trump against Iran. The world has already lost more than 10 per cent of its oil sup...

item.title: Cartoon Highlights of April Fool’s Day in the Philippines
item.description: The latest collection of cartoons compiled by Pitik Bulag for Rappler captures the playful spirit of April Fool’s Day as experienced in the Philippines. Cartoonists use humor and satire to reflect on ...

item.title: PDEA 9 arrests two drug traffickers and seizes P3.4M shabu in Ipil, Zamboanga Sibugay
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Agents from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) 9 successfully apprehended two drug traffickers and confiscated P3.4 million worth of shabu during a standoff in Ipil, Zamboan...

item.title: Prime Minister Albanese outlines Australia's response to Middle East conflict and fuel concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will deliver a pre-recorded address to the nation at 7pm AEDT on Wednesday regarding Australia's response to the ongoing war in the Middle East. The government is monit...

item.title: Pakistan Maintains Essential Goods Supply Despite Middle East Tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed satisfaction over Pakistan’s effective handling of essential commodities amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Chairing a review meeting, he highlighted tha...

item.title: Zanzibar MPs pledge active role in Union Parliament, focus on ports, transport, and joint fund
item.description: Newly sworn-in MPs from Zanzibar have made strong commitments to play an active role in the Union Parliament, aiming to push forward reforms on key issues like port management, transport systems, and ...

item.title: Met Office issues warning for high winds across parts of the UK this Easter weekend
item.description: The Met Office has issued a yellow warning for very strong winds across several regions of the UK over the Easter weekend. The alert is in effect from 6pm on Saturday until midday Sunday, covering Sco...

item.title: Tanzania’s economy grows steadily at 6% in Q3 2025, driven by investment and infrastructure
item.description: Dodoma – Tanzania economy show strong growth trend as it expand by 6.0 per cent in third quarter of 2025, up from 5.5 per cent in same period of 2024. Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba announced this tod...

item.title: How Watching Béla Tarr’s 7.5-Hour Epic Restored Faith in Our Attention Span
item.description: John Semley’s Wired article explores the experience of watching Béla Tarr’s seven-and-a-half-hour film *Sátántango* in a Manhattan theater and how it challenged modern notions of dwindling attention s...

item.title: Diamond Platnumz urges investment in Tanzania's music and entertainment venues
item.description: Tanzanian superstar Diamond Platnumz has raised concern over the shortage of modern entertainment arenas in Tanzania. In a recent interview with Wasafi, he pointed out that while football infrastructu...

item.title: Government Plans Phased Redevelopment of Gikomba Market Without Evictions
item.description: President William Ruto has assured Kenyans that the ongoing redevelopment of Gikomba Market will not involve evictions, with traders fully on board the plans. Speaking at a Maendeleo ya Wanawake meeti...

item.title: Prime Minister urges calm as fuel prices rise due to international conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese addressed the nation in a rare televised message, urging Australians to remain calm amid rising fuel prices caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Speaking ...

item.title: Top international football stars missing from 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup dey near, e don confirmed say some of football's biggest stars no go fit feature for the tournament. Dis na because their national teams no qualify, even though these player...

item.title: Update on Canadian Small-Cap Stocks: Goeasy, Algoma Steel, and High Liner
item.description: Canadian small-cap stocks are showing notable movements today, with Goeasy Ltd., Algoma Steel Group Inc., and High Liner Foods Inc. drawing attention. Goeasy reported a fourth-quarter net loss of $336...

item.title: Trump comments on possibility of U.S. leaving NATO, says Telegraph
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly indicated that the United States is strongly considering withdrawing from the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), according to the Telegraph newspa...

item.title: Experts Question Trump's Claim of Regime Change in Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump has asserted that regime change has occurred in Iran following US-Israeli strikes, claiming that American officials are now dealing with more 'reasonable' actors. However, ex...

item.title: Chancellor outlines targeted energy bill support based on household income
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves has indicated that government support for rising energy bills, driven by disruptions in oil and gas supply from the Middle East due to the Iran war, will be targeted at househ...
